Toh 1.note: Mahīśāsaka Vinaya (五分律, Buddhajīva tr.) parallels Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinayavastu. Cross-school Vinaya parallel. Frauwallner, The Earliest Vinaya and the Beginnings of Buddhist Literature (1956).

Toh 6.note: Dharmaguptaka Vinaya (四分律, Buddhayaśas tr.) parallels Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinayakṣudrakavastu. Cross-school Vinaya parallel. Frauwallner (1956); Clarke, Vinaya Texts (2014).

Toh 1.note: Sarvāstivāda Vinaya (十誦律, Kumārajīva/Puṇyatāra tr.) parallels Mūlasarvāstivāda Vinayavastu. Cross-school Vinaya parallel. Frauwallner (1956).

Toh 334.note: [questionable] Mahāprajñāpāramitopadeśa → Nandimitrāvadāna. The connection between these texts is unclear. Toh 334 is an avadāna narrative, not a Prajñāpāramitā text.

Toh 3786.note: Mahāprajñāpāramitopadeśa (大智度論, Kumārajīva tr.) is a commentary on the Prajñāpāramitā. Toh 3786 (Abhisamayālaṃkāra) is also a Prajñāpāramitā commentary. Both are part of the Prajñāpāramitā commentarial tradition. Conze (1978).
